---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

# Set Up 
```{r}

library(tidyverse)
```


# Reading in Data

```{r}


column_names <- read_delim("data/raw/galton_heights_197_families.txt", 
                           n_max = 1,
                           delim = ",",
                           col_names = FALSE,
                           show_col_types = FALSE)

column_names_vec <- unlist(column_names, use.names = FALSE)



raw_data <- read_delim("data/raw/galton_heights_197_families.txt", 
                       delim = ",",
                       skip = 21, 
                       na = "*",
                      col_names = column_names_vec)
```

# Reading in data directly from HistR

Not sure if we can do this because its already clean. If we need to do it manually, use raw data already uploaded in Github 

source: https://r-packages.io/datasets/GaltonFamilies
```{r}
library(HistData)


data(GaltonFamilies)
str(GaltonFamilies)

```
